Justina Tate, Esq. (619.321.6200; Justina.Tate@wilsonelser.com) of Wilson Elser...& Dicker LLP for Defendants, Uber, et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT ALISON CAMPOS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.
Allison Marie Campos' demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. The FACC adequately pled implied indemnity, contribution and apportionment under a breach of contract theory. (Id. at 7:17-8:6, 10:21-12:12; see also Prince v. Pacific Gas & Electric Co. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 1151, 1165.) No matter how unlikely or improbable, a pleading's allegations must be accepted as true for purposes of ruling on a demurrer. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) The test is whether the complaint states any valid claim, not whether it is the claim intended. (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39.) Moreover, "if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled," so other theories need not be addressed here. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.)

Order is signed.
